1.template: and it was in the year that {0} was the chief judge and the governor over the land

1.title: Six hundred years
1.text: The chapter opens exactly 600 years after Lehi left Jerusalem — the prophetic deadline for Christ's coming. That is why the unbelievers mocked: the time had passed and Samuel's sign had not come... yet.

1.question: What did the unbelievers decree against the believers?
options: That they would be put to death if Samuel's sign did not come by a set day
That they would lose their lands

1.template: for behold, the time is at hand, and on this night shall the {0} be given

1.title: 'On the morrow come I into the world'
1.text: In one of the most singular moments in scripture, Christ himself — not yet born — speaks to Nephi on the eve of his birth: 'on this night shall the sign be given, and on the morrow come I into the world.'

1.question: What sign marked that it was the day the Lord would be born?
options: The night had no darkness, as light as mid-day

1.template: And it came to pass also that a new {0} did appear, according to the word

1.question: Who disturbed the peace, dwelling in the mountains?

2.question: Why did the people begin to doubt the signs they had seen?
options: They began to forget them and harden their hearts

2.title: So quick to forget
2.text: Just a few years after the miracle of the night with no darkness, the people were already 'less and less astonished' at signs and began to ascribe them to 'men and the power of the devil.' How quickly they forgot is one of the book's central themes.

2.template: the Nephites began to reckon their time from this period when the sign was given, or from the coming of {0}
for the {0} robbers had become so numerous

2.question: What happened in the war against the robbers in the fifteenth year?
options: Because of the Nephites' iniquity, the robbers gained many advantages

3.question: What tone did Giddianhi use in his epistle to Lachoneus?
options: He praised his firmness but demanded his surrender

3.title: The robber's letter
3.text: Giddianhi writes to Lachoneus in a curious way: he praises his firmness and 'noble spirit,' yet in the same breath demands he surrender his lands and threatens to exterminate him. It is a blend of flattery and intimidation typical of the secret combinations.

3.template: And behold, I am {0}; and I am the governor of this the secret society of Gadianton

3.question: How did Lachoneus, a just man, react to the robber's threat?
options: He did not yield, but had the people cry to the Lord for strength

3.template: the great commander of all the armies of the Nephites was appointed, and his name was {0}

3.title: Captains who were prophets
3.text: It was the custom among the Nephites to choose as commander someone with the spirit of revelation and prophecy. So Gidgiddoni, the chief commander, was also 'a great prophet' — war strategy was born of revelation.

3.question: What strategy did Gidgiddoni adopt against the robbers?
options: Not to attack, but to gather the people and wait for the robbers to come
To attack them in the mountains

3.question: What did Gidgiddoni have the people do as they gathered?
options: Make weapons and strengthen themselves with armor and shields

4.question: Why did the Nephites gather in one body with seven years of provisions?
options: To survive and destroy the robbers, who had no way to get food

4.template: and they had a lamb-skin about their loins, and they were dyed in {0}

4.title: The robbers' terrifying appearance
4.text: For battle, Giddianhi's robbers appeared with a lamb-skin about their loins, shorn heads, and bodies dyed in blood — 'great and terrible' was their look. But the Nephites did not fear men; they feared God.

4.template: he was overtaken and slain. And thus was the end of {0} the robber

pairs.right: Robber leader, slain in the first battle

4.question: What decision did Zemnarihah make amid the continual destruction during the siege?
options: He ordered to abandon the siege and march northward

4.template: And their leader, {0}, was taken and hanged upon a tree

4.question: How did the Nephites react to the victory over the robbers?
options: They broke forth in song, crying Hosanna to the Most High God

4.title: Victory ascribed to repentance
4.text: After the victory, the Nephites did not boast of their strength: they wept for joy and acknowledged they had been delivered 'because of their repentance and their humility.' The credit was given entirely to God.

5.title: No one doubted
5.text: After Christ's signs and the great deliverance, 'there was not a living soul' among the Nephites who doubted the prophets' words. It was a rare moment of unanimous faith — which, sadly, would not last.

5.question: What was done with the captured robbers who repented?
options: They were set free after covenanting to murder no more

5.question: Why does Mormon say this book cannot contain everything?
options: Not even a hundredth part of what happened in 25 years fits in the book

5.template: And behold, I am called {0}, being called after the land of Mormon, the land in which Alma did establish the church

5.title: Who Mormon is
5.text: Here the compiler of the whole book speaks in the first person: 'I am a disciple of Jesus Christ... a pure descendant of Lehi.' He was named after the land of Mormon, where Alma had established the church, and it is from him that the Book of Mormon takes its name.

5.template: I am Mormon, and a pure descendant of {0}

5.question: What promise does Mormon make about the descendants of Jacob?
options: That the Lord will gather them from the four quarters of the earth
That they will always be few
That they will never know the Redeemer
That they will stay in Jerusalem

5.template: And then shall they know their {0}, who is Jesus Christ, the Son of God

6.question: What happened when the Nephites returned to their lands (years 26-27)?
options: They prospered, with great order and laws of equity and justice

6.template: And there were many {0} cast up, and many roads made, which led from city to city

6.question: How did the people begin to be distinguished in the twenty-ninth year?
options: By ranks, according to riches and chances for learning

6.title: The church breaks up
6.text: In just a few years of prosperity, pride divided the people into classes by wealth, and the church 'was broken up in all the land' — except among a few converted Lamanites, firm and immovable in the faith.

6.template: the redemption which the Lord would make for his people, or in other words, the {0} of Christ

6.question: What did the wicked judges do to those who testified of Christ?
options: They seized and secretly executed them, against the law

6.question: What did the judges and their allies conspire to do at the chapter's end?
options: Destroy the governor and set up a king

6.title: The devil's ancient covenant
6.text: The judges and their allies revived a secret covenant 'given and administered by the devil' — the same Gadianton combination — to unite against all righteousness, destroy the governor, and set up a king. It is the coup that sets up the crisis of 3 Nephi 7.

7.question: What happened to the Nephite government in the thirtieth year?
options: It was destroyed; the people split into tribes

7.title: The end of the government
7.text: Unable to set up a king, the conspirators murdered the chief judge and the Nephite government simply collapsed. The people splintered into tribes, each led by a chief — the end of the era of the judges that had lasted over ninety years.

7.template: did gather themselves together, and did place at their head a man whom they did call {0}

7.question: How did the people organize in the thirty-first year?
options: Into tribes with strict laws, but stoning the prophets

7.template: and even his {0} did he raise from the dead, after he had been stoned

7.title: Nephi raises his brother
7.text: Amid the apostasy, Nephi preached with such power that angels ministered to him daily. In the name of Jesus he cast out devils and — the high point — raised his own brother, who had been stoned and slain by the people, from the dead.

7.question: What did Nephi require of those who repented?
options: That they be baptized with water as a witness

7.question: What happened at the start of the thirty-third year?

8.question: What sign did the people anxiously await?
options: Three days of darkness, prophesied by Samuel

8.title: The exact date
8.text: The text records the precise moment of the cataclysm: the fourth day of the first month of the thirty-fourth year. It was the sign of Christ's death, prophesied by Samuel — three hours of tempest followed by three days of darkness.

8.template: And the city of Moroni did sink into the depths of the {0}, and the inhabitants thereof were drowned

8.question: Where was the most terrible destruction?
options: In the land northward, whose whole face was changed

8.template: all these great and terrible things were done in about the space of {0} hours

8.title: Three hours, three days
8.text: All the devastation — earthquakes, fires, sunken cities — lasted only about three hours. Then came darkness so thick the people could 'feel the vapor of darkness' and could kindle no fire at all, for three days.

8.question: How long did the darkness last, and what did the people do?
options: Three days; there was great mourning and howling

9.title: The voice in the darkness
9.text: Amid the darkness, all the inhabitants heard a voice — that of Christ himself — pronouncing 'wo, wo, wo' and naming, one by one, the destroyed cities. It was the first time the people heard the Savior's voice before seeing him.

pairs.right: Sunk in the depths of the sea
Covered with earth, became a mountain

9.question: Why was the city of Jacobugath burned?
options: For its murders and secret combinations

9.template: Yea, verily I say unto you, if ye will come unto me ye shall have {0} life

9.title: 'I am Jesus Christ'
9.text: For the first time in the Book of Mormon, the resurrected Savior speaks directly, declaring: 'I am Jesus Christ the Son of God... I am Alpha and Omega.' He announces that the law of Moses is fulfilled in him and asks for a broken heart instead of sacrifices.

9.question: What sacrifice does Christ ask, ending the burnt offerings?
options: A broken heart and a contrite spirit

9.template: whoso repenteth and cometh unto me as a {0}, him will I receive

10.template: how oft have I gathered you as a {0} gathereth her chickens under her wings

10.title: The silence
10.text: After the voice, a silence fell that lasted many hours — the people were so astonished they ceased mourning their dead. It was a solemn pause between the destruction and the promise of healing.

10.question: What happened in the morning, after the three days of darkness?
options: The darkness dispersed and mourning turned to joy

10.question: Who was spared from the destruction?
options: The more righteous part, who received the prophets

10.template: Yea, the prophet {0} did testify of these things, and also Zenock spake concerning these things

10.title: The promise of an appearance
10.text: Mormon closes the chapter with an announcement: soon after his ascension into heaven, Christ 'did truly manifest himself' to the survivors, showing them his body. It is the cue for the book's central event, in 3 Nephi 11.

11.question: Where was the multitude gathered when this event took place?
options: Around the temple, in the land Bountiful

11.title: The small voice
11.text: Before seeing the Savior, the multitude heard a voice from heaven three times. It was neither harsh nor loud, but so piercing it made their hearts burn — and only on the third time could they understand it.

11.template: they saw a Man descending out of heaven; and he was clothed in a {0} robe

11.title: They thought it was an angel
11.text: When the Man in the white robe descended and stood in their midst, the multitude dared not open their mouths — they supposed it was an angel. Only when he stretched forth his hand and spoke did they understand it was Jesus Christ himself.

11.template: that ye may thrust your hands into my side, and also that ye may feel the prints of the {0} in my hands

11.question: What did the multitude cry on touching Christ's wounds?
options: Hosanna! Blessed be the name of the Most High God

11.template: Having authority given me of Jesus Christ, I {0} you in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost

11.question: Who does Christ say is the father of contention?
options: The devil, who stirs men to contend with anger

11.question: According to Christ's doctrine, what happens to those who believe and are baptized?
options: They shall be saved and inherit the kingdom of God
They shall be kings on earth

11.template: whoso buildeth upon this buildeth upon my {0}, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against them

11.title: 'Build upon my rock'
11.text: Christ declares his doctrine — repentance, faith, baptism — and warns that declaring 'more or less than this' comes of evil. Whoever follows it builds upon his rock; the gates of hell shall not prevail against them.

11.question: What did Christ command on concluding the declaration of his doctrine?
options: Declare his words to this people, unto the ends of the earth

12.template: now the number of them who had been called, and received power and authority to baptize, was {0}

12.title: The Nephite Beatitudes
12.text: Here Christ gives the Nephite people the Beatitudes — 'blessed are the poor in spirit... the meek... the pure in heart' — nearly identical to those of the Sermon on the Mount in Matthew 5, but with additions directed to the twelve disciples he had chosen.

pairs.right: Shall be called the children of God

12.template: I give unto you to be the {0} of the earth; but if the salt shall lose its savor

12.title: Salt and light
12.text: As in the Sermon on the Mount, Christ calls his followers 'the salt of the earth' and 'the light of this people' — a city on a hill that cannot be hid — urging them to let their good works shine to the glory of the Father.

12.question: What did Christ say about the law and the prophets?
options: He came not to destroy them, but to fulfill

12.question: What teaching did Christ give beyond 'thou shalt not kill'?
options: That whoever is angry with his brother is in danger of judgment

12.template: whosoever looketh on a woman, to lust after her, hath committed adultery already in his {0}

12.question: What did Christ teach about making oaths?
options: That one should not swear at all
That one should swear by heaven
That one should swear by the head

12.template: whosoever shall smite thee on thy right {0}, turn to him the other also
love your {0}, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you
Therefore I would that ye should be {0} even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect

12.title: 'Be perfect even as I'
12.text: The sermon culminates in the command to be perfect. Unlike Matthew 5:48 — which says only 'as your Father' — here the resurrected Christ includes himself: 'even as I,' for he had now lived a perfect life and risen.

13.question: How does Christ teach to give alms?
options: In secret, without a trumpet, so the Father who sees in secret rewards

13.title: Doing good in secret
13.text: Christ teaches that alms, prayer, and fasting should be done 'in secret' — not to be seen of men. Whoever seeks the glory of men 'has his reward already'; the Father who sees in secret will reward openly.

13.template: After this manner therefore pray ye: Our {0} who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name

13.title: The Nephite Lord's Prayer
13.text: Christ gives the Nephite people the Lord's Prayer, nearly identical to that of Matthew 6 — but it omits the petition for 'daily bread' and closes with 'for thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the glory, forever.'

13.question: What does Christ teach about forgiveness?
options: If we forgive men their trespasses, the Father will also forgive us
That one must forgive seven times

13.template: But lay up for yourselves treasures in {0}, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt

13.question: What example does Christ use to teach not to worry about clothing?
options: The lilies of the field, arrayed more than Solomon in his glory

13.template: But seek ye {0} the kingdom of God and his righteousness

14.title: 'Judge not'
14.text: Christ opens this part of the sermon with 'Judge not, that ye be not judged,' adding that with the same measure we mete it shall be measured to us. It is the same teaching as Matthew 7.

14.question: What does Christ teach about judging one's brother?
options: First remove the beam from one's own eye before the mote in the brother's
That one should never correct anyone

14.template: Ask, and it shall be given unto you; seek, and ye shall find; {0}, and it shall be opened unto you
Because strait is the gate, and {0} is the way, which leadeth unto life

14.title: Known by their fruits
14.text: Christ warns against false prophets, who come 'in sheep's clothing' but are 'ravening wolves.' The test to know them is simple: 'by their fruits ye shall know them' — a good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit.

14.question: How are false prophets known, according to Christ?
options: By their fruits — a good tree brings forth good fruit

14.question: What will Christ say to those who say 'Lord, Lord' but work iniquity?
options: I never knew you; depart from me

14.template: shall be likened unto a foolish man, who built his house upon the {0}

15.question: What does Christ declare about the law of Moses?
options: That he gave it and that it is fulfilled in him, having an end

15.title: 'I am he that gave the law'
15.text: Christ clarifies what had confused the people: the law of Moses is fulfilled in him because it was he, the Lord of Israel, who gave it. The 'old things' have passed away, but he does not destroy the prophets — the covenant is not yet wholly fulfilled.

15.template: Behold, I am the law, and the {0}

15.question: How does Christ describe the twelve disciples and the Nephite people?
options: Disciples and a light to the people, a remnant of the house of Joseph

15.template: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, and one {0}

15.title: The 'other sheep'
15.text: In Jerusalem, Jesus had said: 'Other sheep I have which are not of this fold' (John 10:16). Here he reveals he was referring to the Nephites — and those at Jerusalem did not understand, supposing he spoke of the Gentiles.

15.question: Why did those at Jerusalem not understand the saying 'other sheep'?
options: They thought it referred to the Gentiles, because of their unbelief
They did not speak the language

16.question: Why does Christ say he will go to 'other sheep' who have not heard his voice?
options: That there may be one fold and one shepherd

16.title: Written for the latter days
16.text: Christ commands that these words be written and preserved for the Gentiles of the latter days — so that through them the scattered remnant of Israel may be brought back to a knowledge of their Redeemer.

16.template: And blessed are the {0}, because of their belief in me

16.question: What does Christ say will happen if the Gentiles do not turn to him?
options: They shall be as salt that has lost its savor, trodden by the house of Israel

16.template: And then the words of the prophet {0} shall be fulfilled, which say:

16.title: Christ quotes Isaiah
16.text: Christ closes this prophecy by quoting Isaiah 52: 'The Lord hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations; and all the ends of the earth shall see the salvation of God' — tying the Nephites' destiny to the redemption of all the house of Israel.

17.question: What did Christ say he would do on leaving the people that day?
options: Go to the Father and show himself to the lost tribes of Israel

17.title: 'My bowels are filled with compassion'
17.text: Seeing the multitude in tears, not wanting him to leave, Jesus delayed his departure. 'My bowels are filled with compassion towards you,' he said — and invited all the sick and afflicted to be healed.

17.template: Bring them hither and I will {0} them, for I have compassion upon you

17.question: Why did Jesus groan and was troubled before praying for the people?
options: Because of the iniquity of the people of the house of Israel

17.question: How did the multitude describe Jesus' prayer to the Father?
options: Things so great no tongue can speak nor heart conceive

17.template: they saw the heavens open, and they saw angels descending out of heaven as it were in the midst of {0}

17.title: Angels and children
17.text: After blessing the little children one by one, Jesus said: 'Behold your little ones.' Angels descended from heaven in the midst of fire and encircled the little ones, ministering to them — before about 2,500 witnesses.

18.question: What did Jesus do with the bread before giving it to the disciples?
options: He took it, broke it, and blessed it

18.title: The institution of the sacrament
18.text: Here Christ institutes the sacrament among the Nephites: he blesses the bread and wine and commands it be done always, in remembrance of him. It is the same ordinance he had given the apostles at Jerusalem, at the Last Supper.

18.template: ye shall do it in remembrance of my {0}, which I have shed for you

18.title: In remembrance of him
18.text: The bread is taken in remembrance of Christ's body; the wine, in remembrance of his shed blood. The promise is clear: 'if ye do always remember me ye shall have my Spirit to be with you.'

18.question: What does Christ warn about Satan?
options: That he desires to sift the people as wheat; therefore they must always pray
That he will come in person

18.template: Pray in your {0} unto the Father, always in my name

18.question: What does Christ command regarding administering his flesh and blood?
options: Not to let anyone partake unworthily
To administer them to all without exception

18.question: What to do with one who is unworthy, but is not cast out of the synagogues?
options: Continue to minister and pray for him, for he may repent

18.template: he departed from them, and ascended into {0}

18.title: The ascension
18.text: On finishing his instructions, Christ touched each disciple, giving them power to confer the Holy Ghost. Then a cloud overshadowed the multitude and he ascended into heaven — ending the first day of his ministry among the Nephites.

19.question: Which of the twelve disciples had Nephi raised from the dead?

19.title: Twelve bodies
19.text: The multitude was so great that the twelve disciples divided it into twelve bodies, each taught by one of them — a scene of careful organization before the great prayer and baptism.

19.template: And they did pray for that which they most desired; and they desired that the {0} should be given unto them

19.question: What happened to the disciples after they were baptized?
options: The Holy Ghost fell upon them and they were filled with the Spirit and fire

19.template: and they did pray unto Jesus, calling him their Lord and their {0}

19.title: They prayed to Jesus
19.text: In a singular moment, the disciples 'did pray unto Jesus, calling him their Lord and their God.' Since he was present among them, they prayed directly to him — something Jesus himself explains to the Father just after.

19.template: and behold they were as {0} as the countenance and also the garments of Jesus

19.question: For whom did Jesus pray to the Father?
options: For the disciples and all who would believe their words, that they be one

19.question: What did Jesus say about that people's faith, compared to the Jews'?
options: He had never seen so great faith among all the Jews

19.question: What does Jesus tell the multitude about what they saw and heard?
options: None of the Jews saw or heard such great things
That they would forget it all

20.question: What did Jesus do again in this chapter, with the bread and wine?
options: He broke the bread, blessed it, and gave it to the people
He cast them into the sea
He stored them in the temple

20.title: Bread and wine from nothing
20.text: Neither the disciples nor the multitude had brought bread or wine; yet Christ 'truly gave unto them bread to eat, and also wine to drink.' It was a miracle of provision, akin to the multiplying of the loaves.

pairs.right: Eating of Christ's body to the soul
Drinking of Christ's blood to the soul

20.question: What will happen when the words of Isaiah are fulfilled?
options: The scattered remnants of Israel will be gathered from the four quarters

20.template: and ye shall be among them as a {0} among the beasts of the forest

20.question: Who does Christ say he is, quoting Moses?
options: The prophet like Moses whom the Lord would raise up

20.template: then fulfilleth the Father the covenant which he made with {0}, saying:

20.question: What will the people of Israel do when the fulness of the gospel is preached to them?
options: They will believe in Jesus Christ and pray to the Father in his name
They will build a temple in Jerusalem
They will return to the law of Moses

20.template: Awake, awake again, and put on thy strength, O {0}

20.title: 'The Father and I are one'
20.text: Quoting Isaiah 52's prophecy of the redemption of Jerusalem, Christ applies it to the latter-day gathering and adds: 'the Father and I are one' — uniting his work with the Father's in restoring Israel.

20.question: How will Israel go out from the nations in the gathering, according to the prophecy?
options: Not in haste nor by flight, for the Lord will go before them

20.question: What does Christ promise about Jerusalem at the chapter's end?
options: It will be inhabited again by his people, as the land of their inheritance
It will be given to the Gentiles

21.question: What does Christ promise to do, giving a sign to know the time?
options: Gather his people from their long dispersion and establish his Zion

21.title: The sign of the gathering
21.text: Christ gives a precise sign: when 'these things' — the Nephite record — come forth to the Gentiles, it is the sign that the Father's work of gathering Israel has begun. Many understand this as the coming forth of the Book of Mormon in the latter days.

21.template: I will show unto them that my wisdom is greater than the cunning of the {0}

21.question: What will happen to those who will not believe the words of Jesus Christ brought to the Gentiles?
options: They will be cut off from the covenant people
They will inherit the New Jerusalem

21.question: What does Christ say will be done away in that day among the people?
options: Lyings, envyings, strifes, priestcrafts, and whoredoms

21.template: that they may build a city, which shall be called the {0}

21.title: The New Jerusalem
21.text: Christ prophesies that the remnant of Jacob, with the repentant Gentiles, will build a city called the New Jerusalem — a center of gathering where 'the power of heaven shall come down' and he himself will be in the midst.

21.question: When will the work of the Father commence among all the dispersed of Israel?
options: When this gospel is preached to the remnant of this people

22.title: Isaiah 54
22.text: This entire chapter is Isaiah 54, quoted by Christ. It is a song of comfort to Israel — pictured as a barren, forsaken woman who will have more children than the married wife, gathered by the Lord 'with everlasting kindness.'

22.template: and thy {0}, the Holy One of Israel—the God of the whole earth shall he be called
For the {0} shall depart and the hills be removed

22.question: What does Isaiah promise about the children of Zion?
options: They shall be taught of the Lord, and great shall be their peace

22.template: No {0} that is formed against thee shall prosper

23.template: Yea, a commandment I give unto you that ye search these things diligently; for great are the words of {0}

23.question: What did Jesus command the people about the words of Isaiah?
options: To search them diligently and write them
To hide them from the Gentiles

23.title: 'Great are the words of Isaiah'
23.text: Christ commands them to search Isaiah diligently — and Isaiah is, in fact, the most-quoted prophet in the Book of Mormon. Across the book, dozens of Isaiah's chapters are reproduced and expounded.

23.template: I commanded my servant Samuel, the Lamanite, that he should testify unto this people

23.question: What prophecy of Samuel was missing from the records, which Jesus commanded be written?
options: That many saints arose from the dead and ministered to many
That there would be a famine

24.question: Whom did the Father say he would send to prepare the way?
options: His messenger, before the Lord comes to his temple

24.title: Why Malachi?
24.text: Malachi prophesied in Jerusalem centuries after Lehi left — so the Nephites did not have his words. Christ gives them now, commanding they be written, including the famous prophecy of the messenger of the covenant.

24.template: Bring ye all the {0} into the storehouse, that there may be meat in my house

24.title: 'Prove me now herewith'
24.text: Verse 10 holds one of scripture's best-known promises: to bring the tithes into the storehouse and 'prove' the Lord, who promises to open 'the windows of heaven' and pour out a blessing without measure. It is the one place where God invites being put to the test.

24.question: What promise does the Lord make to those who bring the tithes?
options: To open the windows of heaven and pour out a blessing without measure

24.template: And they shall be mine, saith the Lord of Hosts, in that day when I make up my {0}

25.title: Malachi 4 and Elijah
25.text: This brief chapter is Malachi 4, quoted by Christ. It includes the famous prophecy that Elijah would be sent 'before the great and dreadful day of the Lord,' to turn 'the heart of the fathers to the children' — a promise tied to the work for one's ancestors.

25.template: Behold, I will send you {0} the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord

25.question: What will Elijah do, according to the prophecy?
options: Turn the heart of the fathers to the children and the children to the fathers

26.question: What did Jesus expound to the people?
options: All things, from the beginning to the great and last day of judgment

26.title: Only the lesser part
26.text: Jesus expounded 'all things, from the beginning to the last day' — but Mormon records that 'not even a hundredth part' could be written. The greater things were withheld, to be revealed according to the people's faith.

26.template: and I have written them to the intent that they may be brought again unto this people, from the {0}

26.question: Why did Mormon not write all that was on the plates of Nephi?
options: The Lord forbade it, to try the people's faith

26.template: and they had all things {0} among them
And they who were baptized in the name of Jesus were called the church of {0}

27.question: What did the disciples ask Jesus when he appeared?
options: To tell them the name they should give the church

27.title: The name of the church
27.text: There were disputes among the people over what to name the church. Christ settles it: the church must bear his name, for 'if it be called in my name then it is my church' — provided it is built upon his gospel.

27.question: Why must the church bear the name of Christ?
options: Because with a man's name it would be a man's church; with Christ's, it is Christ's church

27.template: that I came into the world to do the will of my {0}, because my Father sent me

27.title: The gospel of Christ
27.text: Christ defines his gospel in few words: he was sent to be lifted up on the cross and thereby draw all men unto him, to be judged of their works. Repentance, baptism, and enduring to the end sum up the way.

27.template: save it be those who have washed their garments in my {0}

27.question: What does Christ say the disciples must do in his church?
options: The works they have seen him do, they shall also do

27.template: And know ye that ye shall be {0} of this people, according to the judgment which I shall give unto you

27.title: 'Even as I am'
27.text: Telling the disciples they would be judges of the people, Christ poses the question: 'what manner of men ought ye to be?' And answers: 'even as I am' — the highest possible standard for a follower.

27.question: Why is Jesus sorrowful in speaking of the fourth generation?
options: Because they would be led captive and would sell him for silver and gold
Because they would forget the language

28.question: What did nine of the twelve disciples desire of Jesus?
options: To come to his kingdom at age 72, after living to the age of man

28.title: Two desires
28.text: Before departing, Christ asks each disciple what he desires. Nine ask to join him in his kingdom at age 72; but three hesitate, with a desire in their hearts they dared not utter.

28.template: for ye shall never taste of {0}; but ye shall live to behold all the doings of the Father

pairs.right: Will come to Christ at 72 years of age
Shall be changed when Christ comes in glory

28.question: What happened to the three disciples when Jesus touched them?
options: The heavens opened and they were caught up, seeing unspeakable things

28.template: And they were cast into {0} by them who did not belong to the church

28.question: What happened to the three when cast to beasts and furnaces?
options: They received no harm; they played with the beasts as a child

28.title: The Three Nephites, unbreakable
28.text: Like Daniel and the three Hebrews in the Bible, the Three Nephites were cast thrice into a furnace and twice to wild beasts — unharmed; they 'played with the beasts as a child with a suckling lamb.' The prisons were rent in twain.

28.template: And they are as the {0} of God, and if they shall pray unto the Father in the name of Jesus

28.question: What happens to those who will not hearken to the words of Jesus and those he sent?
options: They do not receive Jesus, and will not be received by him at the last day

28.template: and they were sanctified in the {0}, that they were holy

28.title: Mormon's question
28.text: Mormon confesses that, as he wrote, he did not know whether the three had been changed from mortality to immortality. He later 'inquired of the Lord' and learned a change was wrought upon their bodies, so they would not taste death until the judgment.

29.question: What will the coming of these words (the record) to the Gentiles signify?
options: That the Father's covenant with Israel is already beginning to be fulfilled

29.title: When the book comes forth
29.text: Mormon gives a sign to readers: when this record comes to the Gentiles, it is proof that the Lord's covenant with Israel is already beginning to be fulfilled — one must not say the Lord 'delays his coming.'

29.template: ye need not any longer hiss, nor spurn, nor make game of the {0}, nor any of the remnant of the house of Israel

30.template: Turn, all ye {0}, from your wicked ways

30.title: The end of 3 Nephi
30.text: The book of 3 Nephi closes with a direct invitation from Christ, given through Mormon to the Gentiles: to turn from wickedness, come unto him, be baptized, and be numbered with the house of Israel — closing the record of the Savior's ministry among the Nephites.
